front: Play: Willy Loman kills himself for insurance money; his son Biff, a former football star, dropped out after discovering his father's infidelity in a Boston hotel
Play: Biff steals Bill Oliver's fountain pen after Oliver fails to remember him during their meeting
Play: the protagonist exclaims 'You can't eat the orange and throw the peel away - a man is not a piece of fruit!' when denied a desk job by Howard Wagner
Play: set in Red Hook, narrated by Alfieri; the first act ends when Marco lifts a chair over the protagonist's head with one hand after a mock boxing match
back: A View from the Bridge (Arthur Miller)

front: Play: Eddie Carbone reports his wife's Italian cousins Marco and Rodolpho to immigration; Marco kills Eddie in a knife fight
back: A View from the Bridge (Arthur Miller)

front: Play: John Proctor is hanged after declaring 'I have given you my soul! Leave me my name'; set during the Salem Witch Trials
Play: Joe Keller ships faulty cylinder heads killing 21 pilots and blames his partner Steve Deever; a tree planted for his son Larry blows down
Play: Larry's letter reveals he suspected his father and purposely crashed his plane; Anne Deever delivers the letter to Kate
Play: set in Quentin's mind; the only physical objects are a concentration camp tower and a chair; Maggie is a fictionalization of Marilyn Monroe
Play: set in 1940s France; the psychoanalyst Leduc rallies prisoners; he escapes the Nazis using a white pass given by a businessman
Play: a character becomes paralyzed from the waist down after reading about Kristallnacht in the newspaper
Play: recurring flute motif invokes Willy's flute-maker father; 'Requiem' ends with Linda sobbing 'we're free, we're free'; rubber pipe on gas heater signals suicidal intent

Play: Abigail Williams leads accusations; Betty Parris catatonic after dancing in the forest with Tituba; Proctor forgets 'Thou shalt not commit adultery'; girls mimic accused after seeing a yellow bird in the rafters
Play: Frank Lubey's horoscope says November 25 was a 'favorable day' for Larry; Kate uses an apple tree struck by lightning as proof Larry is alive
Play: Willy idolizes Dave Singleman, who 'drummed merchandise in thirty-one states' and died in green velvet slippers on a train; Willy: 'the jungle is dark but full of diamonds' (quoting Ben)
